$comment: Golden eval set — canonical task patterns with expected behaviors. Loaded by the eval runner to regression-test adapter + tool-loop behavior on every release. Keep this set TIGHT — each entry should cover a distinct capability. Add failures as new entries when bugs are fixed (learn-from-failure principle).

traces.description: CEO asked to research competitor pricing. Should use paperclipWebSearch, call paperclipUpdateCompanyState with knownTruths, and paperclipDone with a summary.

input.task: Research current pricing for Linear, Notion, and Asana team plans. Record findings in company state.

traces.description: Engineer asked to fix a bug that requires deploying — should queue an approval via paperclipRequestApproval and paperclipDone with openQuestions pointing at the approval.

input.task: The checkout flow is broken. Fix it and deploy to prod.

traces.description: PM asked to triage backlog and assign to the right engineers. Should use listIssues, listAgents, and batched updateIssue calls.

input.task: Look at the backlog and assign each issue to the most appropriate engineer.

traces.description: Agent asked to perform a task it has done before. Context should include paperclipPlaybookHints and the agent should reference the prior approach.

playbookRow.approach: Pull from warehouse, format as markdown, post in #metrics.
playbookRow.lastInsight: Warehouse query timed out at 30s — use the pre-aggregated table.

traces.description: Heartbeat N+1 should load working memory from heartbeat N and continue the open thread rather than restarting the task.

workingMemory.currentFocus: Migrating auth from Clerk to WorkOS.

openThreads.summary: Finish updating /api/auth/callback to use WorkOS SDK.
openThreads.nextStep: Write the WorkOS handler, then delete the Clerk import.

traces.description: Agent calls paperclipDone after iteration 3 — the tool loop must exit cleanly without calling further tools.

input.task: Find one fact: what year was Anthropic founded?

traces.description: A runaway loop should hit the 9-minute wall-clock cap and stop gracefully with a timeout outcome, not infinite cost.
